author: alex-jw-brooks
body: this looks like a bug to me 😞 I pulled `trust_remote_code` out to a top level kwarg in init to pass it through and passed it through from CLI overrides, and switched the fallback/default value to False in both places since we should never have trust_remote_code as a default

author: hsliuustc0106
body: This can return endpoint restrictions for a pipeline that is not the one the server will actually run. `get_pipeline_config()` returns the auto-detected registry match here, but `_create_from_registry()` later reloads `deploy_config_path` and can switch to `deploy_cfg.pipeline`; for those deploy YAMLs we'd reject/allow endpoints based on the wrong pipeline. Can we resolve restrictions from the final pipeline after deploy overrides, or add a regression test for deploy pipeline overrides?

author: alex-jw-brooks
body: Thanks for the fast review @hsliuustc0106! And good point. Yes, I will fix this and add a test for this case tomorrow 🙂

author: yuanheng-zhao
body: Not sure if it's good to cache hf config here, as PretrainedConfig might be mutable in some cases.

author: yuanheng-zhao
body: There might exist potential risks: The function signature changed from `create_from_model(cls, model, cli_overrides, ...)` to `create_from_model(cls, model, trust_remote_code, cli_overrides, ...)`. For now callers use keywords args, but any subsequent or modifications on previous callings with positional args might accidentally bind trust_remote_code to cli_overrides

author: yuanheng-zhao
body: This debug msg seems unsuitable for the scope and purpose of this function (out of scope, we couldn't tell the fallback method inside this func)

author: yuanheng-zhao
body: Why not applying `vllm.entrypoints.serve.utils.error_response.create_error_response` to build err msg

author: alex-jw-brooks
body:
This function isn't used outside of this file and is only used for resolving the model type / pipeline config. The reason I had added the cache is to avoid a second network call when its called a second time, since I wanted to avoid refactoring the messy parts of stage config as much as possible in this pr 🙂

Since its only called here, can we keep it in this pr, and I can submit a follow-up to clean up the config factory? Then we can decouple this feature from the refactor

author: yuanheng-zhao
body: Gotcha. Then it's good to place here. Thanks for clarifying!

author: alex-jw-brooks
body:
Yeah, that's a good point! I do think we need to pull things like this out over `cli_overrides` etc over time though, since the types in the current code are so loose.

For now, I've updated the signature to only allow `model` to be passed positionally so that we don't do this on accident. Does that sound reasonable to you?

author: alex-jw-brooks
body: Yup, good point! I've updated it to make sense with the current call flow

author: alex-jw-brooks
body:
I had initially written it this way since the route is being replaced, not the server handler. So we also have to dump the response immediately like this, which felt a little clunky:
```python
    async def rejection_handler(raw_request: Request):
        error = create_error_response(message=reason)
        return JSONResponse(
            content=error.model_dump(),
            status_code=error.error.code,
        )
```
But I think it probably is a better idea to do this way in case we ever need the sanitization etc, so made the switch!
